| 2 |\n131\t| Utility Functions | 4 |\n132\t| **Total** | **60+** |\n133\t\n134\t## Testing Patterns\n135\t\n136\t### mockBackend Pattern\n137\t\n138\tThe `mockBackend` struct (lines 20-193 in dashboard_test.go) implements the `backend.Backend` interface for testing:\n139\t\n140\t```go\n141\ttype mockBackend struct {\n142\t    objects        map[string]*backend.ObjectInfo\n143\t    commonPrefixes []string\n144\t    listErr        error\n145\t    headErr        error\n146\t}\n147\t```\n148\t\n149\t**Key Features:**\n150\t- **In-memory storage**: Uses `map[string]*backend.ObjectInfo` for objects\n151\t- **Virtual folder support**: `commonPrefixes []string` simulates S3 CommonPrefixes\n152\t- **Error injection**: `listErr` and `headErr` fields simulate backend failures\n153\t- **Zero dependencies**: No actual S3/minio/backend connection required\n154\t\n155\t**Common Usage Pattern:**\n156\t```go\n157\tmb := newMockBackend()\n158\tmb.objects[\"test/file.txt\"] = &backend.ObjectInfo{\n159\t    Key:          \"test/file.txt\",\n160\t    Size:         100,\n161\t    LastModified: time.Now(),\n162\t    IsARMOREncrypted: true,\n163\t    Metadata: map[string]string{\n164\t        \"x-amz-meta-armor-version\": \"1\",\n165\t        // ... other ARMOR metadata\n166\t    },\n167\t}\n168\tmb.commonPrefixes = []string{\"folder1/\", \"folder2/\"}\n169\t\n170\tm := metrics.NewMetrics()\n171\td := New(mb, \"test-bucket\", m)\n172\t```\n173\t\n174\t### httptest.NewRecorder Pattern\n175\t\n176\tStandard Go HTTP testing pattern used throughout:\n177\t\n178\t```go\n179\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/dashboard\", nil)\n180\trec := httptest.NewRecorder()\n181\t\n182\td.Handler()(rec, req)\n183\t\n184\tif rec.Code != http.StatusOK {\n185\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected status 200, got %d\", rec.Code)\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\tbody := rec.Body.String()\n189\t// Validate response body\n190\t```\n191\t\n192\t**Content Validation Patterns:**\n193\t1. **String contains check**: `strings.Contains(body, \"expected text\")`\n194\t2. **JSON decode**: `json.NewDecoder(rec.Body).Decode(&resp)`\n195\t3. **Header validation**: `rec.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")`\n196\t\n197\t### Table-Driven Tests\n198\t\n199\tUsed for multiple test cases (e.g., formatBytes, formatUptime):\n200\t\n201\t```go\n202\ttests := []struct {\n203\t    n        int64\n204\t    expected string\n205\t}{\n206\t    {0, \"0 B\"},\n207\t    {1024, \"1.0 KB\"},\n208\t    {1048576, \"1.0 MB\"},\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n212\t    result := formatBytes(tt.n)\n213\t    if result != tt.expected {\n214\t        t.Errorf(\"formatBytes(%d) = %q, want %q\", tt.n, result, tt.expected)\n215\t    }\n216\t}\n217\t```\n218\t\n219\t## Remaining Test Gaps\n220\t\n221\t### ⚠️ Behaviors Needing Coverage\n222\t\n223\t#### 1. Edge Cases & Boundary Conditions\n224\t| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n225\t|----------|----------|-------|\n226\t| Very large bucket (1000+ objects) | Medium | Performance test |\n227\t| Deep nesting (10+ levels) | Low | Breadcrumb handling |\n228\t| Unicode/special characters in paths | Medium | Already has basic special char test |\n229\t| Very long object keys (255+ chars) | Low | S3 limit is 1024 |\n230\t\n231\t#### 2. Error Recovery & Resilience\n232\t| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n233\t|----------|----------|-------|\n234\t| Backend recovery after transient error | High | List error → retry → success |\n235\t| Partial object data handling | Medium | Corrupted metadata |\n236\t| Concurrent modification handling | Medium | Object changes during request |\n237\t\n238\t#### 3. Security & Input Validation\n239\t| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n240\t|----------|----------|-------|\n241\t| Path traversal attempts | High | `../../../etc/passwd` in prefix |\n242\t| XSS in object keys | High | `<script>` in key names |\n243\t| SQL injection in prefix | Medium | Though we use S3, not SQL |\n244\t| Very long query strings | Low | DoS prevention |\n245\t\n246\t#### 4. |\n|-----------|------------|\n| Basic Rendering | 4 |\n| Object Display & Filtering | 8 |\n| Navigation | 4 |\n| Metrics & Stats | 8 |\n| Authentication | 7 |\n| Admin Operations | 7 |\n| API Endpoints | 6 |\n| Error Handling | 4 |\n| Health & Canary | 3 |\n| Content-Type | 3 |\n| Concurrency | 2 |\n| Utility Functions | 4 |\n| **Total** | **60+** |\n\n## Testing Patterns\n\n### mockBackend Pattern\n\nThe `mockBackend` struct (lines 20-193 in dashboard_test.go) implements the `backend.Backend` interface for testing:\n\n```go\ntype mockBackend struct {\n    objects        map[string]*backend.ObjectInfo\n    commonPrefixes []string\n    listErr        error\n    headErr        error\n}\n```\n\n**Key Features:**\n- **In-memory storage**: Uses `map[string]*backend.ObjectInfo` for objects\n- **Virtual folder support**: `commonPrefixes []string` simulates S3 CommonPrefixes\n- **Error injection**: `listErr` and `headErr` fields simulate backend failures\n- **Zero dependencies**: No actual S3/minio/backend connection required\n\n**Common Usage Pattern:**\n```go\nmb := newMockBackend()\nmb.objects[\"test/file.txt\"] = &backend.ObjectInfo{\n    Key:          \"test/file.txt\",\n    Size:         100,\n    LastModified: time.Now(),\n    IsARMOREncrypted: true,\n    Metadata: map[string]string{\n        \"x-amz-meta-armor-version\": \"1\",\n        // ... other ARMOR metadata\n    },\n}\nmb.commonPrefixes = []string{\"folder1/\", \"folder2/\"}\n\nm := metrics.NewMetrics()\nd := New(mb, \"test-bucket\", m)\n```\n\n### httptest.NewRecorder Pattern\n\nStandard Go HTTP testing pattern used throughout:\n\n```go\nreq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/dashboard\", nil)\nrec := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\nd.Handler()(rec, req)\n\nif rec.Code != http.StatusOK {\n    t.Errorf(\"Expected status 200, got %d\", rec.Code)\n}\n\nbody := rec.Body.String()\n// Validate response body\n```\n\n**Content Validation Patterns:**\n1. **String contains check**: `strings.Contains(body, \"expected text\")`\n2. **JSON decode**: `json.NewDecoder(rec.Body).Decode(&resp)`\n3. **Header validation**: `rec.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")`\n\n### Table-Driven Tests\n\nUsed for multiple test cases (e.g., formatBytes, formatUptime):\n\n```go\ntests := []struct {\n    n        int64\n    expected string\n}{\n    {0, \"0 B\"},\n    {1024, \"1.0 KB\"},\n    {1048576, \"1.0 MB\"},\n}\n\nfor _, tt := range tests {\n    result := formatBytes(tt.n)\n    if result != tt.expected {\n        t.Errorf(\"formatBytes(%d) = %q, want %q\", tt.n, result, tt.expected)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Remaining Test Gaps\n\n### ⚠️ Behaviors Needing Coverage\n\n#### 1. Edge Cases & Boundary Conditions\n| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n|----------|----------|-------|\n| Very large bucket (1000+ objects) | Medium | Performance test |\n| Deep nesting (10+ levels) | Low | Breadcrumb handling |\n| Unicode/special characters in paths | Medium | Already has basic special char test |\n| Very long object keys (255+ chars) | Low | S3 limit is 1024 |\n\n#### 2. Error Recovery & Resilience\n| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n|----------|----------|-------|\n| Backend recovery after transient error | High | List error → retry → success |\n| Partial object data handling | Medium | Corrupted metadata |\n| Concurrent modification handling | Medium | Object changes during request |\n\n#### 3. Security & Input Validation\n| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n|----------|----------|-------|\n| Path traversal attempts | High | `../../../etc/passwd` in prefix |\n| XSS in object keys | High | `<script>` in key names |\n| SQL injection in prefix | Medium | Though we use S3, not SQL |\n| Very long query strings | Low | DoS prevention |\n\n#### 4. Integration Scenarios\n| Behavior | Priority | Notes |\n|----------|----------|-------|\n| Rotation during active requests | High | State coordination |\n| Metrics counter overflow | Medium | 64-bit rollover |\n| Template parse error recovery | Low | Fallback rendering |\n\n### ✅ Behaviors Already Covered (from bf-2qe2q)\n\nAll behaviors from bf-2qe2q acceptance criteria are covered:\n- ✅ Page renders at root → `TestDashboardHandler`\n- ✅ Objects listed correctly → `TestDashboardHandler`\n- ✅ Folder links navigate via ?prefix= → `TestCommonPrefixLinksNavigateByPrefix`\n- ✅ Breadcrumbs link back up hierarchy → `TestBreadcrumbLinksNavigateBack`\n- ✅ Empty bucket renders sanely → `TestEmptyBucket`\n\n## Testing Approach & Guidelines\n\n### Test Naming Conventions\n\n```\nTest[HandlerName][Scenario]          // e.g., TestDashboardHandlerWithPrefix\nTest[Feature][Condition]             // e.g., TestCanaryStatusHealthy\nTest[Endpoint][Auth]                 // e.g., TestMetricsHandlerWithAuth\nBenchmark[HandlerName]              // e.g., BenchmarkDashboardHandler\n```\n\n### Test Structure Guidelines\n\n1. **Arrange-Act-Assert Pattern**:\n   ```go\n   // Arrange: Set up mock backend, metrics, dashboard\n   mb := newMockBackend()\n   // ... populate objects\n   \n   // Act: Make request\n   req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/dashboard\", nil)\n   rec := httptest.NewRecorder()\n   d.Handler()(rec, req)\n   \n   // Assert: Verify response\n   if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {\n       t.Errorf(\"Expected status 200, got %d\", rec.Code)\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Error Testing**:\n   - Always test both success and failure paths\n   - Use `mb.listErr = errors.New(\"...\")` for error injection\n   - Verify appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 404, 500, etc.)\n\n3. **Authentication Testing**:\n   - Test without auth (should fail with 401)\n   - Test with valid auth (should succeed)\n   - Test with invalid auth (should fail with 401)\n   - Test all auth methods (Basic Auth, Bearer token, none)\n\n### When to Add New Tests\n\nAdd tests when:\n1. ✅ Adding new dashboard features or endpoints\n2. ✅ Fixing bugs in dashboard handlers\n3. ✅ Adding new authentication methods\n4. ✅ Changing metrics or stats displayed\n5. ⚠️ Adding edge case handling (currently gaps)\n6. ⚠️ Adding security validation (currently gaps)\n\n### Test Maintenance\n\n- **Run tests before committing**: `go test ./internal/dashboard -v`\n- **Check coverage**: `go test ./internal/dashboard -cover`\n- **Update tests when changing handlers**: Mock behavior must match real behavior\n- **Keep tests independent**: Each test should set up its own mockBackend state\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe dashboard module has **excellent test coverage** for all core behaviors. The 60+ tests provide strong confidence in:\n- Basic rendering and object display\n- Navigation and filtering\n- Metrics and statistics\n- Authentication and authorization\n- Admin operations (key rotation)\n- Error handling\n\n**Remaining gaps** are primarily in:\n- Edge cases and boundary conditions\n- Security and input validation\n- Error recovery scenarios\n\n**Recommendation**: Focus on adding security and edge case tests before addressing other gaps.\n\n## References\n\n- **Test file**: `internal/dashboard/dashboard_test.go` (2016 lines)\n- **Implementation**: `internal/dashboard/dashboard.go`\n- **Previous inventory**: bead bf-2qe2q (closed 2026-07-09)\n- **Backend interface**: `internal/backend/backend.go`\n","numLines":343,"startLine":1,"totalLines":343}}}

component, return the value\n353\t\t\tif i == len(parts)-1 {\n354\t\t\t\treturn current, true\n355\t\t\t}\n356\t\n357\t\t\t// Prepare for next iteration - current might not be a map, which will be caught next iteration\n358\t\t}\n359\t\n360\t\t// Should not reach here if path is non-empty\n361\t\treturn nil, false\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t// isString checks if a value is a string type.\n365\tfunc nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n\t\t\tActualType:   \"float\",\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetRequiredBool retrieves a boolean field value, returning an error if missing or not boolean.\n// Returns the boolean value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not boolean.\nfunc GetRequiredBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (bool, error) {\n\tvalue, err := GetRequiredField(data, path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\n\tif b, ok := value.(bool); ok {\n\t\treturn b, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n\t\tExpectedType: \"boolean\",\n\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateRequiredFields checks that all required field paths exist in the YAML data.\n// Returns a list of missing field paths. Empty list means all required fields are present.\n//\n// Each path in requiredFields uses dot notation for nested access.\n// Fields with nil values are considered missing.\nfunc ValidateRequiredFields(data map[string]interface{}, requiredFields []string) []string {\n\tvar missing []string\n\n\tfor _, path := range requiredFields {\n\t\tif !HasField(data, path) {\n\t\t\tmissing = append(missing, path)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn missing\n}\n\n// FieldRequirement is defined in config.go to consolidate configuration types.\n\n// ValidateFieldRequirements validates field requirements with type checking.\n// Returns a list of validation errors. Empty list means all requirements passed.\n//\n// For each requirement:\n// - If Required is true and field is missing → FieldNotFoundError\n// - If field exists but type doesn't match → TypeMismatchError\nfunc ValidateFieldRequirements(data map[string]interface{}, requirements []FieldRequirement) []error {\n\tvar errors []error\n\n\tfor _, req := range requirements {\n\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, req.Path)\n\n\t\t// Check if field is missing\n\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n\t\t\tif req.Required {\n\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, &FieldNotFoundError{FieldPath: req.Path})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip type check if \"any\" type is specified or no type specified\n\t\tif req.Type == \"\" || req.Type == \"any\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Type validation\n\t\ttypeMatch := false\n\t\tswitch req.Type {\n\t\tcase \"string\":\n\t\t\ttypeMatch = isString(value)\n\t\tcase \"int\", \"integer\":\n\t\t\ttypeMatch = isInt(value)\n\t\tcase \"bool\", \"boolean\":\n\t\t\ttypeMatch = isBool(value)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Unknown type specification - skip validation\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !typeMatch {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\t\t\tFieldPath:     req.Path,\n\t\t\t\tExpectedType:  req.Type,\n\t\t\t\tActualType:    fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// getFieldAtPath navigates through nested map structures using a dot-separated path.\n// Returns the value at the path and a boolean indicating if it exists.\nfunc getFieldAtPath(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, bool) {\n\tif data == nil || path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, false\n\t}\n\n\t// Split path into components\n\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n\n\t// Start with the root map\n\tcurrent := interface{}(data)\n\n\t// Navigate through each level\n\tfor i, part := range parts {\n\t\t// Current level should be a map\n\t\tcurrentMap, ok := current.(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Path component doesn't exist or parent isn't a map\n\t\t\treturn nil, false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get the next level\n\t\tcurrent, ok = currentMap[part]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Field doesn't exist at this level\n\t\t\treturn nil, false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If this is the last component, return the value\n\t\tif i == len(parts)-1 {\n\t\t\treturn current, true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Prepare for next iteration - current might not be a map, which will be caught next iteration\n\t}\n\n\t// Should not reach here if path is non-empty\n\treturn nil, false\n}\n\n// isString checks if a value is a string type.\nfunc isString(value interface{}) bool {\n\t_, ok := value.(string)\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// isInt checks if a value is an integer or float64 representing an integer.\nfunc isInt(value interface{}) bool {\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase int, int64, int32:\n\t\treturn true\n\tcase float64:\n\t\treturn v == float64(int(v))\n\tcase float32:\n\t\treturn v == float32(int(v))\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// isBool checks if a value is a boolean type.\nfunc isBool(value interface{}) bool {\n\t_, ok := value.(bool)\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// GetFieldWithType retrieves a field value and provides type information.\n// Returns the value, a boolean indicating if it exists, and a string describing its type.\nfunc GetFieldWithType(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, bool, string) {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, false, \"missing\"\n\t}\n\tif value == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, true, \"nil\"\n\t}\n\treturn value, true, fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value)\n}\n","numLines":402,"startLine":1,"totalLines":402}}}

nesting\n119\t\t// - Circular references\n120\t\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n121\t\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n122\t\n123\t\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n127\t\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n128\t\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n129\t\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n130\t\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n131\t)\n132\t\n133\t// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\n134\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n135\t\treturn string(v)\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\n139\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n140\t\tswitch v {\n141\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n142\t\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n143\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n144\t\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n145\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n146\t\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n148\t\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n150\t\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n151\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n152\t\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n153\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n155\t\t}\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n159\t//\n160\t// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n161\t// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\n162\ttype ErrorCode string\n163\t\n164\tconst (\n165\t\t// File error codes\n166\t\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n167\t\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n168\t\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n169\t\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n170\t\n171\t\t// Parse error codes\n172\t\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n173\t\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n174\t\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n175\t\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n176\t\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n177\t\n178\t\t// Validation error codes\n179\t\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n180\t\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n181\t\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n182\t\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n183\t\n184\t\t// Schema error codes\n185\t\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n186\t\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n187\t\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n188\t\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n189\t)\n190\t\n191\t// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n192\t//\n193\t// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n194\t// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\n195\ttype ParseError struct {\n196\t\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n197\t\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n198\t\tColumn state\n201\t\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n202\t\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n203\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n204\t\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n205\t\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n206\t}\n207\t\n208\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n209\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n210\t\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n211\t\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n212\t\t}\n213\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n217\tfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n218\t\treturn pe.ErrorType\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n222\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n223\t\treturn pe.ContextStr\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// Error implements the error interface.\n227\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n228\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n229\t\n230\t\t// Build base error with location\n231\t\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n232\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n233\t\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n234\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t} else {\n237\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\t// Add message\n241\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n242\t\n243\t\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n244\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n245\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n246\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n247\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n248\t\t\t}\n249\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n250\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n251\t\t\t}\n252\t\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n253\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\treturn sb.String()\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t// the given parameters.\n267\t//\n268\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n269\t// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n270\t//\n271\t// Parameters:\n272\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n273\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n274\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n275\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n276\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n277\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n278\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n279\t//\n280\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n281\t//\n282\t// Example usage:\n283\t//\n284\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n285\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n286\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n287\t\terrorCode := code\n288\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n289\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n290\t\t}\n291\t\n292\t\treturn &ParseError{\n293\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n294\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n295\t\t\tLine:      line,\n296\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n297\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n298\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n299\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n300\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n301\t\t}\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n305\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n306\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n307\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n311\t//\n312\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n313\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n314\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n315\ttype ValidationError struct {\n316\t\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n337\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n338\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n339\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n340\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n341\t\t}\n342\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n343\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n344\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n345\t\t}\n346\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n365\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t} else {\n368\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", a required field error.\n457\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n458\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n459\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n460\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n461\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n462\t\t\t\treturn true\n463\t\t\t}\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn false\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n471\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n472\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n473\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n474\t\t\t\treturn Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n//\n// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\ntype ParseErrorVariant string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n\t// - Invalid indentation\n\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n\t// - Improper quoting\n\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n\t// expected Go type, including:\n\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Value out of allowed range\n\t// - String length violations\n\t// - Pattern matching failures\n\t// - Required field violations\n\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n\t// including:\n\t// - File not found\n\t// - Permission denied\n\t// - Read/write failures\n\t// - File system errors\n\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n\t// - Invalid nesting\n\t// - Circular references\n\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n\t//\n\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n\treturn string(v)\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n\tswitch v {\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error codes\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, and error code.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue)\nfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode) *ValidationError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n\t\tErrorCode: err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file if an error indicates a file was not found.\n// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\nfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// Check wrapped errors\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = `TestIsInt/uint`\n- `TestIsInt/uint64`\n- `TestIsInt/uint32`\n\n**Symptom:** `isInt(42) = false, expected true` for various integer types\n\n**Root Cause:** The `isInt()` function in `internal/yamlutil/debug_helpers.go:371` only handles:\n- `int`\n- `int64`\n- `int32`\n- `float64`\n- `float32`\n\nIt does NOT handle:\n- `int16`\n- `int8`\n- `uint`\n- `uint64`\n- `uint32`\n\n**Failure Location:** Production code\n\n**Code Location:** `internal/yamlutil/debug_helpers.go:371-382`\n\n**Required Fix:** Add missing type cases to `isInt()` switch statement:\n```go\nfunc isInt(value interface{}) bool {\n    switch v := value.(type) {\n    case int, int64, int32, int16, int8:  // ADD int16, int8\n    case uint, uint64, uint32, uint16, uint8:  // ADD unsigned types\n        return true\n    case float64:\n        return v == float64(int(v))\n    case float32:\n        return v == float32(int(v))\n    default:\n        return false\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Category 2: Required Int String Parsing (1 failure)\n\n**Test:** `TestGetRequiredInt_EdgeCases/string_that_parses_as_int`\n\n**Symptom:** Expected 123, got 0 (string `\"123\"` should parse as integer 123)\n\n**Root Cause:** The `GetRequiredInt()` function in `internal/yamlutil/debug_helpers.go:193` does NOT handle string type values that can be parsed as integers.\n\nThe regular `GetInt()` function DOES handle this correctly (lines 94-99), but `GetRequiredInt()` is missing this case.\n\n**Failure Location:** Production code\n\n**Code Location:** `internal/yamlutil/debug_helpers.go:193-232`\n\n**Required Fix:** Add string parsing case to `GetRequiredInt()`:\n```go\ncase string:\n    // Try to parse string as integer\n    if i, err := strconv.ParseInt(v, 10, 64); err == nil {\n        return int(i), nil\n    }\n    return 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n        FieldPath:   path,\n        ExpectedType: \"integer\",\n        ActualType:   \"string\",\n    }\n```\n\n---\n\n### Category 3: Error Type Detection (3 failures)\n\n**Tests:**\n- `TestGetYAMLErrorType/ParseError_returns_ErrorTypeParse`\n- `TestGetYAMLErrorType/SchemaValidationError_returns_ErrorTypeSchema`\n- `TestGetYAMLErrorType/wrapped_ParseError_returns_ErrorTypeParse`\n\n**Symptoms:**\n- `GetYAMLErrorType() = \"\", expected \"parse\"`\n- `GetYAMLErrorType() = \"schema_validate\", expected \"schema\"`\n\n**Root Cause:** Test code directly constructs error structs WITHOUT using constructor functions, leaving required fields uninitialized.\n\n**Example from test (lines 95-97):**\n```go\n{\n    name:     \"wrapped ParseError returns ErrorTypeParse\",\n    err:      fmt.Errorf(\"wrapped: %w\", &ParseError{FilePath: \"test.yaml\"}),\n    expected: ErrorTypeParse,\n}\n```\n\nThe test creates `&ParseError{FilePath: \"test.yaml\"}` directly, which leaves `ErrorType` field as empty string (`\"\"`). The constructor `NewParseError()` would set `ErrorType: ErrorTypeParse` (line 298).\n\nSimilarly, `SchemaValidationError` uses `ErrorTypeSchemaValidate` instead of the test's expected `ErrorTypeSchema`.\n\n**Failure Location:** Test code (tests need to use constructor functions)\n\n**Code Location:** `internal/yamlutil/errors_test.go:95-97`\n\n**Required Fix:** Update test to use constructor functions OR update expected values:\n```go\n// Option A: Use constructor (RECOMMENDED)\nerr: fmt.Errorf(\"wrapped: %w\", NewParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"msg\", 0, 0, \"\", \"\", \"\"))\n\n// Option B: Fix expected value\nexpected: ErrorTypeSchemaValidate  // instead of ErrorTypeSchema\n```\n\n---\n\n### Category 4: File Error Messages (2 failures)\n\n**Tests:**\n- `TestReadFile/file_not_found`\n- `TestParseYAML/file_not_found_returns_FileError`\n\n**Symptom:** `expected error message to mention 'not found', got: 'file error during read on /nonexistent/path/file.yaml: '`\n\n**Root Cause:** The `FileError` doesn't include the underlying error message in its `Error()` method output.
